So the IOC's main concern was the fragility of the jade rings. The next sentence says the IOC required that jade rings should be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The mint actually raised that to 3 meters. The result was that the medals could survive a 3-meter drop with only minor cracks. This shows that the main issue was the potential for the jade to break or crack because of its fragility. Now looking at the options: A) the medals will be worth less – This isn't mentioned. The text doesn't talk about the value of the medals. B) the medals are too difficult to make – The text does mention that the technology is not easy, but the IOC's specific concern wasn't about the difficulty of making them, but about the jade's fragility.
